plays.change_sketch: 1) start.c:1017: create target_pid_pipe whenever !restart_enabled (drop the !console_mode clause). 2) Add an int target_pid_fd parameter to hold_run_console_broker (console.h decl; adopted entry passes -1); in the broker, immediately after handshake success (broker.c:247-251), hold_write_all(target_pid_fd, &target, sizeof target) and close it; close it in every broker_fail_errno exit before that point. 3) No parent change needed: start.c:1241-1261 already reads the pipe and re-anchors pid, so the record block at 1285-1295 and the identity-token reads at 1389-1390 now describe the target. 4) Optional hardening: a broker SIGTERM handler that forwards TERM to -target and falls into the existing cleanup. 5) Shell test: 'hold --console /bin/sh -c "trap \"\" HUP; sleep 45"', then 'hold end <id>' -> poll: group dead, record state exited with signal-derived code, <id>.sock gone from the store. Verify: make (zero warnings), full 135-test suite, layer lint.

plays.thesis: Treat the HLOGIDX sidecar as a journal that must be validated against its 'superblock' — the raw log's actual size — at every open/load, discarding a stale index instead of trusting it, exactly as jbd2 refuses a journal that disagrees with the filesystem it describes.
plays.improves: Metadata honesty in the viewer and logs paths. The writer already persists raw_size_bytes/raw_mtime_ns into the sidecar header on every append (logging.c:340-341) — the tie-fields exist for exactly this check — but hold_open_log_index_fd (logging.c:169-207) accepts any header that parses (line 189) and hold_logidx_map_load (logging.c:242-303) never compares them to the raw log, so a copytruncate-rotated log yields non-ascending offsets that break the binary search's ascending invariant (logging.c:305-318) and hold_logidx_map_find returns the OLD record for a NEW line: fabricated timestamp and stream label rendered as truth, defeating the viewer's 'never fake metadata' rule (filter.c:115). The play also pins the currently untested crash-recovery rule (min of header count and physical count, logging.c:265-271) with the two cheap tests the ledger asked for.
plays.risks: One extra stat per map load and per writer reopen (negligible). Writer-side rebuild on mismatch discards historical index entries rather than repairing them — deliberate (see objection). The reader-side staleness check needs the raw log's size; hold_logidx_map_load currently opens only the idx, so it must stat log_path too — log_path is already its first parameter, so no signature change.
assumptions: raw_size_bytes written at append time (logging.c:340-341) is a reliable high-water mark: header claims > current raw size implies truncation/rotation, never a benign state.
No test fixture relies on a stale sidecar being accepted — viewer_filter_test.c's build_indexed_log only ever produces healthy sidecars (per the ledger's test audit).
mtime regression is a weaker secondary signal (clock steps) and can be omitted or made advisory; size is the load-bearing check.
plays.strongest_objection: This hardens a corner case — logrotate copytruncate against a live held call's log — that no user has reported, and 'discard, don't repair' throws away valid metadata for all lines before the truncation point. Honest answer: fabricated OUT/timestamp labels presented as fact are worse than absent metadata under Hold's own stated rule, repair is impossible anyway (the offsets no longer address the same bytes), and the jbd2 discipline is precisely that a journal you cannot trust in full you must not trust at all. But if the owner rates rotation-during-hold as out of scope, the test-pinning half of the play still stands alone.
plays.unique_insight: The staleness fields are write-only code today: every append pays the cost of maintaining raw_size_bytes/raw_mtime_ns and no reader ever consults them. Two comparisons — one in hold_open_log_index_fd (fstat of raw_log_fd is already in hand at logging.c:176) and one in hold_logidx_map_load — convert existing dead weight into the mount-check that makes the 'documented, stable format' promise (SPEC.md:50-53) mean something, and give the format doc its crash-recovery clause for free.
plays.change_sketch: 1) hold_open_log_index_fd: after read_logidx_header succeeds (logging.c:189), if h.raw_size_bytes > (uint64_t)st.st_size (st already fstat'ed from raw_log_fd at 176), fall through to the existing ftruncate+fresh-header path (192-206) instead of returning the stale fd. 2) hold_logidx_map_load: stat(log_path); if header raw_size_bytes > raw size, close and return -1 so callers render raw lines without metadata (filter.c already handles map-load failure as no-metadata). 3) viewer_filter_test.c: three cases — (a) truncate raw log, append one line, assert map load rejects; (b) torn third entry, assert count==2; (c) header entry_count inflated to 9, assert physical count wins. 4) docs/store.md: short normative subsection stating header fields and the two rules (min-count recovery, size-tie staleness), superseding the wrong meta-bit layout in docs/future/viewer-fixes-before-0.5.md. Verify: make test (viewer_filter_test runs in suite), zero warnings, 135/135.

plays.source: flight data recorders (no final frame recorded => report 'no data', never interpolate to nominal)
plays.thesis: Adopt the flight-recorder rule for exit status: only a written final frame (state 'exited' via hold_mark_run_finished) may carry an exit code, and a missing final frame must render as the honest 'Exited (?)' — which means the restart supervisor must write its final frame, and the loader must stop resurrecting a fabricated 0 from a running record's State.ExitCode.
plays.improves: Status truthfulness across two confirmed findings. (a) run_restart_supervisor ends with _exit(0) and never calls hold_mark_run_finished (start.c:337-371), and the waiter that would is gated off for restart calls (start.c:1017), so a failed service under --restart shows 'Exited (0)' and inspect stays 'running' forever. (b) The writer emits State.ExitCode 0 for every running record (record.c:97 '(r && r->has_exit_code) ? r->exit_code : 0') and the loader trusts it back unconditionally (record.c:661-665, has_exit_code=true), so list.c's honest branch 'Exited (?)' (list.c:135) is unreachable for any record hold itself wrote: every unmarked death — supervisor killed, power loss — becomes a false clean exit, the exact STATUS phrase docker-parity-contract.md:56-58 reserves for success. Both fixes are sanctioned by normative docs, so behavior change is licensed.
plays.risks: Passing store+id into run_restart_supervisor threads two parameters through the fork site at start.c:1070-1084 (both in scope there); the supervisor is a detached session leader with stdio possibly gone, but hold_mark_run_finished (record.c:972-1015) is pure fd work and is already called from the equally-detached waiter at start.c:1112. Gating the loader's State.ExitCode fallback must not break reading legitimately finished records — gate on Status=='exited' or has_ended_at, both of which mark_run_finished always sets (record.c:990-1006). A hidden test could assert 'Exited (0)' on an unmarked-death path; grep first, and the full suite is the arbiter.
assumptions: hold_mark_run_finished succeeds from the supervisor's context (same uid, record_dir path valid post-setsid — it is an absolute path).
No consumer depends on inspect's ExitCode being 0-when-unknown rather than absent/honest; docker itself reports 0 there, but list's STATUS is the contract surface the ledger flags.
The g_restart_stop break path (start.c:364) has a meaningful last status to record; on 'ended before any worker exited' the supervisor records the TERM-derived status, which is honest.
plays.strongest_objection: The loader gate touches the read path for every record, and 'Exited (?)' surfacing where users previously saw 'Exited (0)' is a visible behavior change that could alarm people whose calls die at reboot — some will read '?' as hold losing data rather than hold refusing to invent it. Honest answer: that is precisely the FDR trade — a question mark is an accurate instrument reading, a fabricated zero is an accident-report falsification; the parity contract's STATUS semantics side with the question mark, and the reboot case already renders as 'Stale' (list.c:142-146), so '?' appears only where death was truly unwitnessed.
plays.unique_insight: The 'Exited (?)' honesty branch at list.c:135 is dead code created by hold's own writer: record.c:97 bakes a 0 into every running record and record.c:661-665 launders it back into has_exit_code on load, so the system fabricates the evidence it later trusts. The fix is not adding honesty — it is removing the two lines of self-deception so the honesty already written can execute, plus making the one supervisor that never files a final report (start.c:371) file it. Inverting 'default 0' to 'default unknown' is the whole play.
plays.change_sketch: 1) start.c: add 'const struct hold_store *store, const char *id' to run_restart_supervisor's signature; before _exit(0) at start.c:371 call hold_mark_run_finished(store, id, status) (retry loop mirroring the waiter at start.c:1111-1117); caller at 1070-1084 passes store/id already in scope. 2) record.c:661-665: take the State.ExitCode fallback only when the loaded Status/state is 'exited' or has_ended_at is set; otherwise leave has_exit_code false so list.c:135 renders 'Exited (?)'. 3) Tests: (a) 'hold -d --restart on-failure:1 --restart-delay 0 /bin/sh -c "exit 3"' -> poll record ended -> list STATUS matches 'Exited (3)', inspect state exited; (b) plant a running-state record whose pid is dead -> list shows 'Exited (?)' not 'Exited (0)'. Verify: make, full 135-test suite (watch for any test asserting the old fabricated 0), layer lint.

plays.thesis: docker-parity-contract.md:9 promises 'Tests assert the shapes written here', but three promised shapes have zero asserting tests: the --env-file flag (contract line 22; grep of tests/ finds no hit), the flagship `hold on`/`hold off` verbs (identity doc's core surface, exercised only via the legacy `shell` alias at test_hold.sh:3705/3728/3775), and the Docker-formatted PORTS IPv6+comma cell (fixture at test_hold.sh:2749 deliberately carries '127.0.0.1:8080/tcp, [::]:9090/tcp' but the assertion at 2755 greps only the IPv4 half). Pin all three so a regression in contract-listed behavior cannot pass 135/135.
plays.improves: Fidelity: a dispatch regression that breaks `on`/`off` while keeping the `shell` alias, a --env-file parser break, or a PORTS join/bracket regression currently ships green. Resonance: directly executes the owner's 'contracts pinned in docs + tests' discipline.
plays.risks: The env tests must not entrench the known -e/--env-file self-setenv bug (cli_main.c:266 mutates hold's own environment; ledger high-severity finding). Mitigation: assert only child-visible env (via logs -p of a `sh -c 'echo $KEY'` call) and the stderr error shapes at cli_main.c:301/319 ('cannot read env file', 'invalid --env-file entry at path:line', rc 5) — shapes that survive the eventual fix, which will keep applying recorded env via apply_child_env.
assumptions: --env-file happy path works today (ledger probe verified it; I read the full implementation at cli_main.c:294-330)
The activation banner string 'Hold is now active...' at src/runtime/shell.c:704-706 is the intended stable shape
`hold off` outside a session returns rc 1 with 'not inside a hold on session' (shell.c:755-767); test env must unset HOLD_ON_PID for determinism
hold-on-identity.md:38-41 sanctions on/off as permanent surface, so pinning them is licensed (unlike the --cap flags, which the cut table orders deleted)
plays.strongest_objection: Tests for behaviors that already work change nothing user-visible, and the --cap findings show this suite has a history of tests entrenching surfaces slated for deletion — am I repeating that mistake? Answer: no for these three, because each is named in a normative doc that survives redline (parity contract line 22 for --env-file, identity doc 38-41 for on/off, parity contract 62-63 for PORTS); the --cap tests were pinning surface the superior doc orders cut. I deliberately do NOT pin `hold list -h`/`ps -h` or the flags-only-usage-to-stdout paths, because the ledger shows those behaviors are contract-violating and pinning them would freeze bugs.
plays.unique_insight: The suite tests the alias but never the canonical verb: all three guarded-shell tests spell it `"$HOLD_BIN" shell`, so the primary `on`/`off` dispatch (cli_main.c:717/760/765) has zero coverage under its documented names — and the PORTS gap is a one-line grep extension because the fixture author already planted the IPv6 port that nothing asserts.
plays.change_sketch: (1) New test_env_file_sets_child_env: write an env file with a comment line, a blank line, and two KEY=VALUE lines; `hold --env-file f -d -- /bin/sh -c 'echo $PROBE_A-$PROBE_B'`; record_ended_soon; assert logs -p prints the joined values. New test_env_file_rejects_bad_entry: file with 'NOEQUALS' at line 3 -> rc 5, stderr matches 'invalid --env-file entry at .*:3'; missing path -> rc 5, stderr 'cannot read env file'. (2) Switch the alias invocation at test_hold.sh:3705 to `"$HOLD_BIN" on` and add `grep -q 'Hold is now active' hold-shell.err`; keep 3728/3775 on the alias spelling as alias coverage until the owner redlines it. New test_off_outside_session_refuses: `env -u HOLD_ON_PID "$HOLD_BIN" off` -> rc 1, stderr 'not inside a hold on session'. (3) Extend the grep at test_hold.sh:2755 to match the full cell '127\.0\.0\.1:8080/tcp, \[::\]:9090/tcp'. Register the three new tests next to their families (~4085+). Verify: make test (138/138).

plays.thesis: SPEC.md:50-53 leans on the .log/.log.idx pair being a 'documented, stable format', yet the sidecar's durability machinery has zero tests and no correct written spec: the crash-recovery rule (trust min(header count, physical count), logging.c:265-271), the 1MiB length clamp that sets HOLD_LOGIDX_META_TRUNCATED (logging.c:327-330), and the actual meta bits (STDERR=0x0001, NO_NEWLINE=0x0002, CONTINUATION=0x0004, TRUNCATED=0x0008, logging.c:13-16) are all unpinned — and the only written byte layout (docs/future/viewer-fixes-before-0.5.md) contradicts the shipped bits. Following the Unix tradition that an on-disk format is proven by an independent second reader, add unit cases to viewer_filter_test.c that decode the sidecar byte-by-byte with locally defined constants, making the test the executable v1 format spec.
plays.improves: Fidelity: a regression in the durability rule SPEC depends on would currently ship silently (build_indexed_log at viewer_filter_test.c:325-348 only ever produces healthy two-entry sidecars). Learnability/extensibility: the independent-decode test becomes the anchor for the missing normative format doc — whoever writes it can transcribe asserted bytes instead of reverse-engineering logging.c, and external readers get a pinned layout.
plays.risks: Byte-level tests double-book the layout constants (80-byte header, 16-byte entries, entry_count at header offset 32, meta in bits 48-63 of the second u64) — if the format legitimately revs to v2, two places need updating. That is the point of the pin, but it must be commented as deliberate double-entry so a maintainer does not 'fix' the test by including logging.c's private defines.
assumptions: The recovery behavior works today (the ledger's compiled probe confirmed torn-entry -> physical count and header overcount -> physical count); these tests pin, not fix

Header layout as read this session: magic@0, version u16@8, header_size u16@10, entry_size u16@12, base_unix_us u64@24, entry_count u64@32, raw_size_bytes u64@40, raw_mtime_ns u64@48 (write_logidx_header, logging.c:94-110); entries: pos_len u64 LE (offset:44 bits, len-1:20 bits) + time_meta u64 LE (delta-us:48 bits, meta u16 high)
make test already builds and runs viewer_filter_test (the macOS lane builds it explicitly at test_macos_ci.sh:99), so new cases ride the existing wiring
plays.strongest_objection: This pins internals of a format whose staleness fields are known-broken (raw_size_bytes/raw_mtime_ns written on every append but never validated on load — a separate high-severity ledger finding), so am I hardening a half-finished design? Partly — but the cases I pin (min-count recovery, TRUNCATED clamp, meta bit positions) are exactly the parts the eventual staleness fix must NOT change, and pinning them first makes that fix safer to land. I deliberately do not assert that stale sidecars are rejected, since that behavior does not exist yet.
plays.unique_insight: The repo has two contradictory byte specs — the shipped single-bit meta flags versus the future-doc's 3-bit stream field — and no test votes for either; a partial stdout line on disk today reads as STDIN under the only written doc. An independent-decoder test with its own constants is the cheapest way to declare which layout is v1, and it simultaneously covers the crash-recovery rule using nothing but ftruncate and one pwrite.
plays.change_sketch: Four new cases reusing build_indexed_log: (1) test_logidx_recovers_torn_entry: append a third line, then ftruncate the .idx to 80+2*16+7 bytes; hold_logidx_map_load must return count==2 and both surviving records intact. (2) test_logidx_recovers_header_overcount: pwrite u64 LE 9 at idx offset 32; reload -> count equals physical (2). (3) test_logidx_truncated_meta_bit: write one (1<<20)+16-byte newline-free chunk via hold_write_indexed_log_bytes_fd; reload -> record len==1<<20 and (meta & 0x0008). (4) test_logidx_v1_byte_layout: with locally #defined IDX_HEADER=80/IDX_ENTRY=16/meta bits (comment: deliberate double-entry, do not include private defines), pread raw entry bytes and assert the stderr entry carries 0x0001 and a partial (no-newline) stdout write carries 0x0002 at bits 48-63 of the second u64, plus offsets ascending. Register all four in main(). Verify: make test, make viewer-filter-test, plus scripts/ci.sh ASan lane locally.

plays.thesis: scripts/ci.sh:2-3 claims to be 'the full local mirror of the GitHub CI matrix', but the mirror runs backwards: GitHub never executes `make test-040` (ci.sh:55) or scripts/check_version_sync.sh (ci.sh:39) — ci.yml:27 and release.yml:52 run only `make test`. test_040 is the only suite exercising the non-HOLD_TESTING release-personality binary (hold-dynamic) plus the -p/-v honest rejections and ps-header smoke, so a regression there ships and even tags a release. Fuse the two gate definitions back into one contract by adding both steps to ci.yml and release.yml — and, because test_040 still uses the fixed-sleep style the main suite already abandoned, harden the five remaining one-shot sleeps in the same play so the new gate does not import flakes.
plays.improves: Fidelity: closes the ship-a-regression gap for the release-personality binary and version sync, and removes the load-dependent false reds that would otherwise make the new gates untrustworthy (a flaky release gate is worse than none — .github/scripts/test_macos_ci.sh:36's one-shot sleep already gates tag publishing via release.yml's build-macos dependency).
plays.risks: CI YAML cannot be fully verified locally; mitigate by running `bash scripts/ci.sh` (which executes the identical commands) plus the three edited test scripts end-to-end locally, and yamllint/actionlint if available. Adding test-040 to two workflows costs ~1 minute of runner time per job.
assumptions: test_040_dockerish.sh's own polling patterns (lines 83-89 and 101-104) are the accepted idiom, so replacing the fixed sleeps at lines 59 and 62 with a poll for '"state": "exited"' in the record JSON matches house style

test_hold.sh's record_ended_soon/pgid_terminated helpers (lines 521-529, 541-553) are the intended replacements for the one-shot sleeps at 599/603 (group-kill children), 1877 (leader-zombie list), and 1888 (finished-tail)
The suite's own hardening history (the TERM-to-KILL race fixes) shows the owner wants polls, not sleeps
plays.strongest_objection: test_040 on shared GitHub runners is a new flake surface and CI minutes cost, and one could argue the honest fix is instead to delete the 'full local mirror' claim from ci.sh. But the claim is the cheaper thing to keep true: the ledger shows real, user-facing behaviors (-p/-v rejection messages, ps header, redial-by-name) guarded only by this script, and the flake objection is exactly why the sleep-to-poll conversion is fused into the same play rather than left as a follow-up. I also explicitly rejected the tempting third gate — shellcheck in CI — because I measured the baseline this session at 46 warnings/36 errors across tests/, scripts/, and .github/scripts/: it would cry wolf from day one, violating the low-noise rule (cppcheck already gates at ci.yml:71-79).
plays.unique_insight: The drift is directional: local CI is a strict superset of GitHub CI, which inverts the usual failure mode — the developer machine is the only place two release-gating checks ever run, so the checks most likely to be skipped under deadline pressure are the only enforcement. Fusing the definitions (same steps in ci.sh, ci.yml, release.yml) makes the mirror claim self-verifying: any future step added to one side shows up as a diff reviewers can see.
plays.change_sketch: (1) ci.yml build-and-test: after `make test` add `make test-040 CC=${{ matrix.cc }} ...` and a leading `bash scripts/check_version_sync.sh` step; release.yml:52 job: add `make test-040`. (2) test_040_dockerish.sh: replace `sleep 0.3` at lines 59 and 62 with a 30x0.1s poll on the run record for '"state": "exited"' (record path derivable from $HOME/.local/state/hold/<full64>.json; the named call's full id is already captured at line 57 before cutting to 12). (3) test_hold.sh test_group_kill_children: wrap the ps child-scan at 600 in a retry loop until non-empty, and replace the one-shot pid_dead_enough at 603-606 with a per-child retry loop mirroring pgid_terminated; convert the sleeps at 1877 and 1888 to grep-with-retry. (4) test_macos_ci.sh:36-41: replace `sleep 0.2` with a 2s retry loop around the logs grep. Verify: bash scripts/ci.sh (runs both suites with the new polls), bash tests/test_040_dockerish.sh standalone x5 for stability, bash -n on all edited scripts.
